Notice Of Hearing On Re Filed Motion And Refiled Motion To Confirm Final Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VINCE TRICARICO, TEL. NO. 1-213-706-0585 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JOHN C. GUGLIOTTA OF GUGLIOTTA & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 914-813-1704 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER NEAL TRICARICO'S Motion To Confirm Final Arbitration Award is GRANTED. The parties' employment agreement contained valid and enforceable language requiring the parties to arbitrate the two claims asserted by petitioner in the arbitration proceedings. As correctly stated in the final award, the arbitrator had the authority to decide whether petitioners' claims were arbitrable. While in retrospect the better procedure might have been for petitioner to have filed a petition to compel arbitration, there is no requirement in California law that he do so. Respondents Luxar Portfolio, Inc. and Michael Webb also could have filed a lawsuit seeking to enjoin the arbitration. Instead respondents chose to participate partially but not fully in the arbitration proceedings, to assert an objection to the arbitrator's jurisdiction, and later to seek to withdraw from the arbitration. Respondents had every right to assert an objection to the arbitrator's jurisdiction and to participate as fully as they wished in the arbitration proceedings, but their decision not to participate fully and to "withdraw" from the arbitration is not a basis to deny confirmation of the arbitration award. Because there is no basis to vacate or correct the arbitration award, it must be confirmed. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

Re Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Con Firm Final Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Vincent Tricarico, Esq. (213-706-0585) appears telephonically as counsel for petitioner, Neal Tricarico

Attorney, John C. Gugiolotta of Gugliotta & Associates (914-813-1704) appears telephonically as counsel for respondents Luxar Portfolio Inc., Michael Webb

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER NEAL TRICARICO'S Petition To Confirm Final Arbitration Award is DENIED without prejudice to re-filing the motion based on the contention that the issues decided by the arbitrator were subject to a binding and enforceable arbitration agreement and/or respondents waived any right they had to object to the arbitrator deciding the issues that she decided. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order tracking, verbatim the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
